mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS reflects continued monetary tightening that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, independent of any threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from 0.13% in 2015 to 1.91% by mid-2018 as Fed hiking cycle progressed
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend slope and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns are positive in >55% of months with rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window from a cycle low, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes broad economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter (60 trading days).
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.3pp over trailing 3 months from a local minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8-3.9% in mid-2018, near multi-decade lows, a level from which reversals historically foreshadow slowdowns
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with historically low unemployment
failure_condition: In periods where the 0.3pp trigger fires, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals that the Fed is tightening into resilient consumer demand, a configuration historically associated with soft-landing optimism and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; the effect requires both conditions jointly, as either alone is ambiguous.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage_value 507533 million in July 2018 with FEDFUNDS at 1.91% amid ongoing hiking cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances show forward 20d returns not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, decelerating INDPRO growth signals approaching capacity constraints and margin compression for industrial firms, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this mechanism is only active when the expansion has already run many years, distinguishing it from early-cycle industrial slowdowns which do not predict equity weakness.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage_value 107.9989 in July 2018 after multi-year expansion since 2009
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (post 2009 recovery, multi-year growth streak)
failure_condition: During INDPRO deceleration episodes in late-cycle windows, 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports consumer income and corporate revenue expectations, producing modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as a continuous, always-on signal.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage_value 149128 thousand persons in July 2018, consistent with continued job gains
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5-day returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST (housing starts) exceeding 10% from its 12-month rolling peak signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and often precedes broader consumer retrenchment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter once the threshold is crossed.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month peak
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value 1173.0 thousand units in June 2018, below prior 2018 peaks near 1300
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising mortgage rates
failure_condition: Following trigger events, 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling validation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising while UNRATE is simultaneously at or near multi-decade lows, the joint configuration indicates the Fed is tightening against a fully employed economy with limited labor slack, raising recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising over trailing 6 months AND UNRATE below 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.91% and UNRATE at 3.8-3.9% in mid-2018, an unusually tight labor market amid active hiking
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle at full employment
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show 20-day forward returns not significantly negative versus baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates above 2.5%, it signals rising input cost pressure and raises the probability of accelerated Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following week as markets reprice rate-hike expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value 251.286 in July 2018, with YoY inflation running near 2.9%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with above-target inflation
failure_condition: During periods when CPI YoY exceeds 2.5%, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02.
